channel.title: Squares on Bell Favorite Recipes
channel.description: Recent content in Squares on Bell Favorite Recipes

item.title: Almond Bars
item.description:
Ingredients

  • Line cookie sheet (ll x l7) with foil. Grease foil.
  • Put graham wafers (single layer) on foil. Top with l/2 pkg of flaked almonds.
  • Boil ¾ cup brown sugar with l cup butter for 4 or 5 minutes.
  • Spoon this mixture over graham wafers and almonds
  • Bake l0 minutes at 350 degrees. Cut them in half while still hot.
  • Graham wafers
  • l/2 pkg flaked almonds
  • ¾ cup brown sugar
  • l cup butter

item.title: Almond Bars
item.description:
Ingredients

  • Line cookie sheet (ll x l7) with foil. Grease foil.
  • Put graham wafers (single layer) on foil. Top with l/2 pkg of flaked almonds.
  • Boil ¾ cup brown sugar with l cup butter for 4 or 5 minutes.
  • Spoon this mixture over graham wafers and almonds
  • Bake l0 minutes at 350 degrees. Cut them in half while still hot.
  • Graham wafers
  • l/2 pkg flaked almonds
  • ¾ cup brown sugar
  • l cup butter

item.title: Butter Mallow Squaraes
item.description:
Instructions

BASE: 1/3 cup butter 1 cup lightly packed brown sugar 1 egg 1 tsp vanilla ¾ cup all-purpose flour 1 tsp baking powder ¼ tsp salt ½ cup chopped pecans (I used walnuts) FILLING: 2-1/2 cups miniature marshmallows TOPPING: ¼ cup butter ½ cup lightly packed brown sugar 2 Tbsp milk 1 cup sifted icing sugar 32 pecan halves (optional) BASE: Melt butter in large saucepan. Stir in brown sugar, egg and vanilla. Blend together flour, baking powder and salt. Stir dry ingredients into butter mixture along with pecans or walnuts. Spread batter in greased 8” square pan. Bake at 350 degrees for 20 – 30 minutes. Immediately scatter KRAFT marshmallows over entire surface of hot cake. Press gently to flatten. TOPPING: Melt butter in saucepan. Add brown sugar and simmer over low heat, stirring constantly for 2 minutes. Add milk, stirring until mixture just return to boil. Remove from heat. Gradually add icing sugar, beating with a wooden spoon after each addition until smooth. If necessary, thin icing with an additional few drops of milk. Spread quickly over top of marshmallows. Decorate with pecans, if desired. Cool before cutting into squares. Makes 20 squares. NOTE: I found squares are easier to cut and remove from pan if they are still a bit warm when I cut them.

item.title: Butter Tart Squares
item.description:
Ingredients

  • Base:
  • 1-1/4 cup flour
  • ¼ cup brown sugar
  • ½ cup butter
  • Filling:
  • 1/3 cup butter
  • 1 egg
  • 2 tbsp cream
  • 1/2 tbsp flour
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • 1/2 cup raisins (or walnuts)
  • 1 cup brown sugar

Instructions

Grease pan lightly, especially around the edges to make it easier to remove edges pieces. Mix base ingredients together and press firmly in a 9-inch square pan. Mix filling together. Pour over base and bake at 350 degrees until done. (25 to 30 minutes)

item.title: Chewy Chip Squares
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1-1/2 cups graham cracker crumbs
  • 1 cup chocolate chips
  • 1 cup butterscotch chips
  • 1 cup chopped walnuts
  • 11-oz sweetened condensed milk

Instructions

Measure all ingredients. Stir together well. Grease a 9 x 9 pan. Smooth with back of spoon. Bake for 30 – 35 minutes in a 350 degree oven. Cool for 4 – 5 minutes. Cut into 36 squares

NOTE: I tried this in a well-greased pan and it was a real struggle to get out of the pan. The next time I will grease the pan, line it with tin foil and grease this very generously and keep my fingers crossed that it will come out easy.

item.title: Chocolate Chip Bars
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup margarine
  • 1 cup brown sugar
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • 2 cups sifted flour
  • 1 cup chocolate chips
  • 1 cup chopped walnuts

Instructions

Cream margarine well. Add sugar and vanilla and mix well. Add flour a little at a time and mix well. Then add chips and walnuts. Pack in a jelly roll pan and bake at 350 degree oven for 20-25 minutes. Cut while warm.

item.title: Chocolate-caramel Squares
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 8-1/2 or 9-oz pkg cream-filled sugar wafer coolies
  • 24 vanilla caramels
  • 2 tbsp milk
  • 6-oz pkg (1 cup) semi-sweet choc.chips 3 tbsp margarine or butter

Instructions

Lightly grease 8”or 9” square pan. Arrange single layer of wafer cookies side by side over bottom of greased pan. Reserve half of wafer cookies for second layer. In small saucepan, melt caramels with milk over low heat, stirring occasionally until well blended.

item.title: Crunchy Fudge Sandwiches
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 6-oz pkg (1 cup) Butterscotch Morsels 1 6-oz (1 cup) Chocolate Chips
  • ½ cup peanut butter
  • ½ cup sifted icing sugar
  • 4 cups Rice Krispies
  • 2 tbsp soft butter or margarine
  • 1 tbsp water

Instructions

Melt Butterscotch Morsels with peanut butter in heavy saucepan over very low heat, stirring until well blended. Remove from heat. Add Rice Krispies; stir until well coated with butterscotch mixture. Press half of cereal mixture into buttered 8 x 8 x 2 pan. Chill in refrigerator while preparing fudge mixture. Set remaining cereal mixture aside. Combine chocolate chips, sugar, butter and water in top of double boiler; place over hot water and stir until chocolate melts and mixture is well blended. Spread over chilled cereal mixture. Spread remaining cereal mixture evenly over top. Press in gently. Chill. Remove from refrigerator for about 10 minutes before cutting into squares.

item.title: Date Squares
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups all-purpose flour
  • 2 cups rolled oats
  • 1 cup brown sugar
  • ½ cup coconut
  • 1-1/4 cup shortening
  • ½ tsp soda
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 1 tsp baking powder
  • 1-1/2 lb dates

Instructions

Rub first 8 ingredients together until crumbly. Put 2/3 mixture in bottom of a 9 x 12 cake pan. Cook dates with 1-1/2 cups water until soft. Add ¼ to ½ cup white sugar and 2 tbsp butter. Mix well. Spread date mixture over crumbs in bottom of pan. Top with remaining crumb mixture. Bake in a 350 degree oven for 30 minutes.

item.title: Easiest Ever Lemon Squares
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup all-purpose flour
  • ¼ cup granulated sugar
  • ½ cup butter
  • 1/3 cup raspberry jam (or your choice)
  • 2 eggs
  • 1 pkg (212 g) Shirriff Lemon Pie Filling
  • ½ cup ground almonds
  • ¼ cup sliced almonds

Instructions

Combine flour and sugar, then butter until the mixture clumps together. Press firmly in 8” square pan. Bake at 350 degrees 15 – 18 minutes or until slightly browned. Remove from oven and spread with jam. Beat eggs until light and creamy. Beat in pie filling and ground almonds. Pour over jam layer. Sprinkle with sliced almonds. Bake 20 –25 minutes or until set and firm to touch. Cool. Cut into squares. Makes 25 squares.

item.title: Golden Graham Bars
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2/3 cup light corn syrup
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • 2 tsp margarine or butter
  • 1 10-oz pkg golden grahams
  • 1 11-oz pkg chocolate chips
  • 3 cup mini marshmallows

Instructions

Butter 13”x 9”x 2” baking pan. Heat syrup, butter and chocolate chips just to boiling in a 3-quart saucepan, stirring constantly. Remove from heat. Stir in vanilla. Pour over cereal in large bowl; toss quickly until coated. Fold in marshmallows, 1 cup at a time. Press in pan. Let stand until firm – about 1 hour.

item.title: Lemon Squares
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup shortening
  • 2 cups flour
  • ½ cup icing sugar

Instructions

Mix until all crumbly. Lightly grease a 9”x 13” pan. Press in pan and bake for 20 minutes at 350 degrees. (Looks watery).

Topping:

4 eggs 2 cups white sugar ½ tsp salt 1 tsp baking powder 4 tbsp lemon juice

Mix (not cook) – pour on crust and bake 18 minutes at 350 degrees.

item.title: Popular Brownie Recipe
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1-1/2 cups all-purpose flour
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 2 cups white sugar
  • ½ cup (8 tbsp) cocoa
  • 2 tsp vanilla
  • 1 cup oil
  • 4 eggs
  • ¼ cup cold water
  • ½ cup (or more) chopped walnuts

Instructions

Put all ingredients in beater bowl in order given ending with walnuts and beat on low speed or by hand until smooth. Do not beat too long. Grease a 9 x 13 pan. Smooth batter in it and bake at 350 degrees for 30 minutes – no longer. Ice if desired.

item.title: Raspberry Chocolate Squares
item.description:
Instructions

These decadent treats have soft coconutty centers, a touch of raspberry and a luscious layer of chocolate on top.

1-1/4 cups all-purpose flour 1/3 cup granulated sugar ½ cup butter 1 egg yolk 1/3 cup raspberry jam 1 can (300 ml) sweetened condensed milk 2 cups sweetened flaked coconut 5 oz semisweet chocolate 1 tbsp butter

In bowl, mix together flour and sugar, using pastry blender or 2 knives, cut in butter until mixture is roughly the size of peas. Stir in egg yolk, mix thoroughly.

item.title: Ribbon Squares
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup melted butter or margarine
  • 1 tsp baking powder
  • 1 cup brown sugar, packed
  • 2 cups quick cooking oatmeal
  • ½ tsp vanilla

Instructions

Mix last four ingredients, add butter and mix. Place on cookie sheet – spread out so sheet is evenly. Bake for approximately 5 minutes at 350 degrees – or until mixture is bubbling. Remove and let sit for 5 minutes. Cut in squares.

item.title: Sonia’s Absolutely Fabulous Dessert
item.description:
Ingredients

  • (1 cup chopped walnuts or pecans
  • 1 Tbsp sugar )
  • ( 1 cup flour
  • ½ cup butter ) 1st Part
  • 1 large tub Cool Whip
  • 1 cup icing sugar
  • 8 oz (250 gr) cream cheese
  • 1 small pkg instant chocolate pudding
  • 1 small pkg instant vanilla pudding
  • 4 cups milk (Separated)
  • 1 Jersey milk chocolate bar

Instructions

Cream together first four ingredients: press in a 9” x 13” pan. Bake at 350 degrees for 15 to 20 minutes. Cool well.

item.title: Swedish Strips
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup shortening
  • l cup brown sugar
  • l egg
  • l tsp vanilla
  • l/4 tsp salt
  • 2 cups all-purpose flour
  • l/2 cup jam
  • 2 tbsp icing sugar
  • l/2 cup nuts, finely chopped

Instructions

Cream shortening and sugar until light and fluffy. Add egg, vanilla and salt and mix well. Add flour and combine. Place cookie batter on slightly greased cookie sheet. Spread and flatten until l/4”thick.

Baking time: l5 minutes at 350 degrees.

item.title: Toffee Chocolate Chunk Almond Bars
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup all-purpose flour
  • ½ tsp baking powder
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • 1 cup Skor chips
  • ¾ cup chocolate chips
  • 1/3 cup sliced natural almonds
  • 1/3 cup unsalted butter, melted
  • ½ cup firmly packed light brown sugar
  • 1 tsp almond extract
  • ½ tsp vanilla extract
  • 1 large egg

Instructions

Preheat over to 350 F. and coat an 8” pan with non-stick spray, or line with non-stick aluminum foil. Whisk flour, baking powder and salt together in a small bowl. Toss together toffee, chocolate chips and almonds in a small bowl. Put one cup of mixture into another small bowl and ½ cup into a third bowl. Whisk together melted butter, brown sugar and extracts in a medium bowl (or pot you melted butter in) until blended. Whisk in egg until well combined. Stir in flour mixture until a few streaks of it remain, then stir in first bowlful of toffee mixture until evenly combined. Spread in an even layer in baking pan and sprinkle top evenly with reserved 1 cup of toffee mixture. Bake until light golden brown around edges but soft in center, 30 minutes; a toothpick inserted in center should come out with wet batter attached. Sprinkle remaining ½ cup of toffee mixture evenly over top. Bake until toothpick inserted in center comes out with a few moist crumbs attached, about 5 more minutes. Place pan on rack to cool completely. Cut into 16 bars. NOTE: Grease pan really well as this recipe tends to really stick, especially around the edges.

item.title: Toffee Frost-ease
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup butter
  • 1 cup brown sugar
  • 2 cups flour
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • 1 cup walnuts
  • 1 cup chocolate chips

Instructions

Cream butter, sugar and vanilla. Stir in flour & nuts and mix well.

Pat into an ungreased 9 x 13 pan. Bake at 350 degrees F. for 15 – 20 minutes, until lightly browned.

Remove from oven and sprinkle chocolate chips evenly over top. After about 5 minutes they’ll be soft – then spread evenly.

item.title: Top Hat Triple-layer Bars
item.description:
Instructions

The tuxedo of dessert bars, this dressed up sweet will become a holiday tradition at your house.

½ cup butter, melted 1-1/2 cups graham cracker crumbs 1 200-gr pkg flaked sweetened coconut 1 tin (300 ml) sweetened condensed milk 1 350-gr pkg semisweet chocolate chips ½ cup smooth peanut butter

Combine butter with cracker crumbs in bowl; press evenly over bottom of 13 x 9 cake pan or glass baking dish lined with parchment paper. Top evenly with coconut, then condensed milk.

item.title: Uncle Paul’s Pumpkin Bars – (creation Unique)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 4 eggs
  • 1-2/3 cups granulated sugar
  • 1 cup cooking oil
  • 16 oz. Can pumpkin
  • 2 cups all-purpose flour
  • 2 tsp baking powder, ground cinnamon
  • 1 tsp salt, baking soda

Instructions

In mixing bowl, beat together eggs, sugar, oil and pumpkin till light and fluffy. Stir together baking powder, cinnamon, salt and soda. Add to pumpkin mixture and mix thoroughly. Spread batter in 2 lightly greased 8” x8” pans.

Bake in 350 F oven for 25 minutes. Cool. Frost bars with your favourite cream cheese frosting.
